-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-a11g","title":"E2E Tests: Dark Mode & Theme Switching","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0qd. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for dark mode and theme switching functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Theme Detection
1. Follows system preference by default
2. Light mode renders correctly
3. Dark mode renders correctly
4. No flash on page load

### Manual Toggle
5. Toggle switch visible
6. Click switches theme
7. Preference persists after refresh
8. Works in logged-out state

### Visual Consistency
9. All components styled for dark mode
10. Images/icons adapt appropriately
11. Charts/graphs readable in both
12. No white flashes during navigation

### Edge Cases
13. System preference changes honored
14. Override persists over system
15. Reset to system option works

## Files to Create
- e2e/theme/dark-mode.spec.ts
- e2e/theme/toggle.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/theme-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- No visual regressions in either mode
- Smooth theme transitions
- Preference correctly persisted

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-a12t","title":"E2E Tests: Accessibility (WCAG, Screen Readers, Keyboard Navigation)","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-pld. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for accessibility compliance.

## Test Scenarios

### Keyboard Navigation
1. Tab order is logical
2. Focus visible on all interactive elements
3. Skip to content link works
4. Modal focus trap works
5. Escape closes modals/dropdowns
6. Arrow keys navigate menus

### Screen Reader
7. All images have alt text
8. Form labels associated correctly
9. ARIA landmarks present
10. Live regions announce changes
11. Error messages announced
12. Loading states announced

### Visual Accessibility
13. Color contrast meets WCAG AA
14. Focus indicators visible
15. Text resizable to 200%
16. No horizontal scroll at 320px
17. Touch targets 44x44px minimum

### Semantic HTML
18. Headings in logical order
19. Lists used appropriately
20. Tables have headers
21. Links vs buttons correct

### Interactive Elements
22. Buttons have accessible names
23. Links describe destination
24. Form validation accessible
25. Tooltips keyboard accessible

## Tools
- Playwright + axe-core for automated checks
- Manual testing with VoiceOver/NVDA

## Files to Create
- e2e/accessibility/keyboard.spec.ts
- e2e/accessibility/screen-reader.spec.ts
- e2e/accessibility/visual.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/a11y-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Zero axe-core violations
- Keyboard-only navigation complete
- Screen reader tested on key flows
